seoTitle: Insert Excel Into a Confluence Page (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to insert an Excel workbook into a Confluence page in 2026, either by uploading a local file or connecting a live OneDrive share link.
intro: Checking a budget in a separate tab breaks focus, and copy-pasted numbers lose their formulas. This guide shows how to insert a Microsoft Excel workbook directly into a Confluence page in 2026 so the data stays live.

steps.heading: Open the file block
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/excel` or `/file` to open the upload block.

steps.heading: Upload from your desktop
steps.body: Click Upload, browse your system, and drag your Excel workbook into the container.

steps.heading: Or link a cloud file
steps.body: Paste a secure Microsoft 365 or OneDrive sharing URL to generate an interactive card instead.

steps.heading: Configure the viewer
steps.body: Click the embedded frame to adjust viewport height, toggle gridlines, and select specific sheets.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review your layout and click Publish to save the settings and go live.

faq.question: Can I insert an Excel file without uploading it to Confluence?
faq.answer: Yes, pasting a OneDrive or SharePoint share link renders the workbook as a live interactive card without a local upload.

faq.question: Will inserting a large workbook slow down the page?
faq.answer: The database architecture handles external spreadsheet streams so grids render without slowing down text loading.

faq.question: Can I choose which sheet tab displays first?
faq.answer: Yes, the embedded viewer's settings let you select a default sheet tab.

faq.question: Does the embedded workbook update if I change the source file?
faq.answer: Yes, when connected via OneDrive or SharePoint, edits to the source file reflect automatically in the embedded view.
